confirmed.note: Three SidePanel setup blocks (m_ShaderSidePanel 630-806, m_OverlaySidePanel 810-896, m_CustomSidePanel 901-1012) each open with the SAME verbatim 10-line brls::Box configuration header (setHideHighlight/setPositionType/setPositionTop/Left/setWidthPercentage/HeightPercentage/setBackgroundColor(0,0,0,60)/setFocusable(false)/setVisibility(GONE)), only the member name differs; bodies diverge (shader params vs overlay path vs custom). Classic copy-paste-and-edit of a panel-builder block.

formatSpeed() copied to formatSize(): identical 1024/1024*1024 ladder + snprintf(buf,"%.1f KB",.../1024.0) structure, only the unit-suffix literals edited (" B/s"->" B", "KB/s"->"KB", "MB/s"->"MB") and the divisor variable type. formatETA below diverges (time formatting) so it is not part of the clone.

false_positives.what_happened: Two brls::Button construction blocks (the cancel button in ProgressDialog ctor and the OK button in showResult()): new brls::Button / setText(...) / setWidth(140.f) / registerClickAction([...]{popActivity; return true;}) / m_buttonBox->addView(btn). They are ~7 near-identical lines because the brls UI framework forces this exact build-button idiom; the differing parts (label text, captured this, extra m_onCancel call) are tiny. A token detector at w>=0.79 flags it but there is no copied logic, only a GUI-widget construction idiom.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add an idiom-suppression filter for short GUI-builder runs — when a candidate clone is <=8 statements AND >=70% of its tokens are method-call chains on a freshly new'd local/member of a single widget type (new X(); x->setA(); x->setB(); container->addView(x);), down-weight or drop it. Equivalently raise the min-token / min-statement threshold for blocks dominated by setter-chain calls so framework boilerplate doesn't cross the report bar.

false_positives.what_happened: Multiple brls::DetailCell button blocks (cancel / manual / close / install): new brls::DetailCell(); cell->setText(...); cell->registerClickAction([](View*){ ...; popActivity(); return true; }); box->addView(cell). Same DetailCell-builder idiom repeated for each action button; only the captured lambda body and label differ. Detector sees high token overlap on the builder skeleton.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: same GUI-builder idiom suppression as above — treat repeated new+setter-chain+registerClickAction+addView sequences on framework widget types as boilerplate, not clones. Could be data-driven via an allowlist of builder method names (setText/setWidth/registerClickAction/addView/registerAction) that, when they dominate a block, exclude it from clone reporting.

false_positives.what_happened: formatSpeed/formatSize/formatETA family share the cascading-threshold-return shape (if x<A return ...; if x<B return ...; return ...). formatETA in particular looks similar to the byte formatters structurally but encodes a totally different unit ladder (seconds->minutes) with different literals. A structural/token detector can over-match the three as one cluster, but formatETA is independent logic, not copied.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: when comparing short threshold-ladder functions, weight numeric-literal and string-literal divergence higher — if the constant set and format-string set differ substantially between two blocks (e.g. 1024 vs 60, "KB" vs "分"), treat them as a units-family idiom rather than a clone. This keeps the real formatSpeed<->formatSize pair (same 1024 constants) while dropping the formatETA false pairing.

cycle.desc: no include cycle: archcheck --graph reports nodes=127 edges=251 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1
cycle.evidence: Author code under src/ and include/ (127 nodes, 6 unresolved, 340 external). No cyclic SCC. Vendored libs (third_party/borealis, extern/mgba, example/gfx retroarch) are excluded from author graph and were not assessed for cycles per scope.
